content.heading: The Brief
content.body: A 34-room boutique hotel targeting the international business traveller needed a lobby that worked as a genuine arrival experience — not a holding area. The client wanted guests to feel the hotel's character within ten seconds of crossing the threshold.

content.caption: The lobby from the entrance — immediate warmth, clear orientation, no wasted space.

items.heading: Concept
items.body: We drew from East African craft traditions — woven textiles, hand-thrown terracotta, carved timber screens — and translated them into a contemporary language. The lobby speaks of place without being a caricature of it.

items.caption: A seating alcove adjacent to the check-in desk — for guests who arrive early or want to wait.

content.heading: Result
content.body: The lobby has become a destination in itself — the hotel now opens its bar and lounge to non-residents, and the space regularly features on travel editorial lists for Kampala. Occupancy moved from 61% to 84% within six months of reopening.
